data.title: Bitcoin Nears ‘Historic’ Technical Test As Price Eyes $93,500 Barrier – What’s Next?
data.description:
As Bitcoin (BTC) breaks out of key resistance levels, an analyst suggests that the cryptocurrency is positioning itself for a move to higher levels and a retest of a crucial technical area in the coming weeks.

Bitcoin Approaching Make-Or-Break Test

On Tuesday, Bitcoin surged 2.5% to retest the $93,500 resistance level for the first time in a week. The cryptocurrency has been hovering between the $84,000 to $93,500 price range for three months and has failed to turn this level into support multiple times.

Analyst Rekt Capital recently noted that the flagship crypto is near a “historic” test as it has begun to form “another technically decisive region” just above current price levels.

The market watcher explained that BTC is approaching its dynamic Bull Market Exponential Moving Average (EMA) cluster, where the 50-week EMA and 21-week EMA are getting closer.

This key cluster, currently located between the $96,000 and $97,500 levels, has historically been tested before a “meaningful crossover,” with the Bitcoin price overextending beyond the cluster.

However, this has usually been followed by an unsuccessful confirmation of this region as support. “When that happens, the crossover itself often follows the bearish price event, rather than causing it, with the EMA cluster flipping into resistance from the underside and leading to downside continuation,” the analyst detailed.

Notably, past cycles reveal that the 50-week and 21-week EMAs can move very close together, Rekt Capital wrote, emphasizing that they can even overlap for prolonged periods before a decisive crossover.

Currently, Bitcoin has yet to retest and overextend beyond the two EMAs, but its historical performance suggests that it will likely occur. Moreover, BTC’s price is “positioning itself in a way that could allow for a springboard higher, potentially enabling a test of this cluster in the weeks ahead. The key question is timing.”

BTC Price Breaks Out Of Key Resistances

In his analysis, the market observer discussed BTC’s recent performance, which has seen a structural change despite the sideways price action. Last week, the cryptocurrency’s price closed above its multi-week downtrend, which has been serving as a major resistance point since late November.

This marks “a small but notable technical milestone” as Bitcoin now holds above the November and December highs in the weekly timeframe, treating the previous resistance as support.

In addition, the mid-zone of its local range, around the $90,500 level, is now “almost perfectly confluent with the former Downtrend, meaning the Downtrend that last week rejected price is beginning to act as layered support instead.”

Therefore, if Bitcoin continues to hold the mid-range region, the price should be able to challenge higher levels and find a path toward $100,000. Rekt Capital added that, unlike previous retests, the most recent rejection from the crucial $93,500 resistance was significantly shallower and shorter, suggesting that it was getting weaker.

Now, the flagship crypto has successfully retested the downtrend breakout area as support and momentarily reclaimed the $93,500 resistance, surging above the $94,000 area once again.

Ultimately, BTC will need to hold this area and close the week above $93,500 to “kickstart a breakout from the Weekly Range as per previous green circles,” the analyst concluded.

As of this writing, BTC trades at $94,334, a 2.6% increase in the weekly timeframe.

data.title: Owlto Finance (OWL) - Gate.io Listing - 15 January 2026
data.description:
According to the listing announcement on X, Gate.io will open OWL spot trading against USDT at 11:00 AM UTC, creating a new centralized exchange market for Owlto Finance. The mechanism is straightforward: listing on a large exchange typically increases accessibility, order-book depth, and arbitrage routes, which can expand short-term trading volume and tighten spreads. This can attract momentum traders and new entrants who could not buy OWL on previous venues. At the same time, improved liquidity also gives early holders a better exit channel, often leading to significant intraday volatility around the listing window.

Gate
@Gate

data.title: Nobody Sausage (NOBODY) - Team Updates X Space - 15 January 2026
data.description:
The Nobody Sausage team will host a live X Space with Nimbus Capital to discuss the project’s future and provide more information on Blind Boxes, as outlined in their Space announcement. Mechanistically, this is a communication and narrative event: management can reveal strategic direction, partnership depth with Nimbus Capital, and the economic role of Blind Boxes for the NOBODY ecosystem. Any new details on token utility, distribution, or revenue flows tied to Blind Boxes could shift fundamental assumptions and sentiment. If the content is perceived as strong, it may trigger speculative inflows; if vague or disappointing, it risks a sell-the-news reaction after the Space.

data.title: Lava Network (LAVA) - 2026 Roadmap Livestream - 15 January 2026
data.description:
The Lava Foundation and contributors will present the 2026 technical roadmap in a live session moderated by Cryptocito, as stated in the official X announcement and the linked YouTube livestream. Mechanically, this is an information event that can clarify upcoming development milestones, infrastructure priorities, and ecosystem plans. Clear, credible timelines and features often improve investors’ conviction and can justify re-rating if expectations were low or vague. Conversely, indications of delays, reduced scope, or high uncertainty can pressure price. Traders should watch for concrete deliverables, funding visibility, and any incentives or staking mechanics that could alter LAVA’s demand and supply dynamics.

Lava Network
@lavanetxyz

Live Stream // 2026 Roadmap

Thu Jan 15, 3pm UTC

Join the Lava Foundation and contributors for an overview of the Lava Network 2026 Technical Roadmap, moderated by @Cryptocito.

️ Hit the reminder on YouTube:https://t.co/deOOJuHvB3 pic.twitter.com/poZL0MbTKl

Jan 13, 2026

data.title: Saks Global files for bankruptcy after Neiman Marcus takeover leads to financial collapse
data.description:
High-end department store conglomerate Saks Global filed for bankruptcy protection late on Tuesday in one of the largest retail collapses since the pandemic.

The move cast uncertainty over the future of U.S. luxury fashion barely a year after a takeover that brought Saks Fifth Avenue, Bergdorf Goodman and Neiman Marcus under the same roof.

The beleaguered luxury retailer was close to finalizing a $1.75 billion financing package with creditors that would allow its stores to remain open, two people familiar with the negotiations told Reuters earlier on Tuesday.

The company's biggest unsecured creditors are Chanel and Gucci owner Kering at about $136 million and $60 million respectively, the court filing said.

A retailer long loved by the rich and famous, from Gary Cooper to Grace Kelly, Saks fell on hard times after the COVID pandemic, as competition from online outlets rose, and brands started more frequently selling items through their own stores.

In 2024, parent company Hudson's Bay bet on scale by merging it with rival Neiman Marcus, creating the entity now known as Saks Global. The $2.7 billion deal was built on about $2 billion in debt financing and equity contributions from investors including Amazon , Salesforce and Authentic Brands.

data.title: China's 2025 oil imports, December inflows both hit record highs
data.description:
Key points:
  • China December and 2025 annual crude oil daily import volumes hit all-time highs
  • Higher crude throughput, firmer restocking demand and low oil prices supported imports, analysts say
  • December natural gas imports rose 16.3% from a year earlier

By Sam Li and Lewis Jackson

China's crude oil imports rose 17% from a year earlier in December, while total imports in 2025 rose 4.4%, government data showed on Wednesday.

In addition, daily crude oil import volumes hit all-time highs in both December and for all of 2025.

The world's largest crude importer brought in 55.97 million metric tons of oil in December, equivalent to 13.18 million barrels per day (bpd), up 10% from 50.89 million tons in November, according to the General Administration of Customs.

China imported 557.73 million tons of crude oil in 2025, or 11.55 million bpd, up 4.4% from a year earlier, the data showed.

The growth in crude oil imports was attributable to stronger crude throughput and firmer restocking demand, according to Kpler, a consultancy. It added that China’s oil throughput is expected to reach 15.38 million bpd in 2025, up 0.7% from a year earlier.

The average stock build in 2025 was 430,000 bpd, up from 84,000 bpd in 2024, with half of the growth driven by new storage capacity from both state-owned and independent companies, according to consultancy Rystad Energy.

“Energy security is the primary driver of China’s stockpiling amid rising geopolitical tensions," said Ye Lin, vice president at Rystad Energy. "Low oil prices also matter, as the average crude cost in China was $10 per barrel lower than in 2024 amid sanctions."

Kpler and ship-tracking firm Vortexa both estimated record-high seaborne crude imports in December of more than 12 million bpd.

Lower oil prices encouraged refiners to increase purchases, while strategic-petroleum-reserve restocking may also have played a role, said Muyu Xu, an analyst at Kpler.

Independent refiners were able to buy more spot cargoes after receiving import quota allocations in November, Xu added.

Kpler and Vortexa said onshore crude inventories rose by 35 million barrels in December and Kpler added that China’s onshore crude oil inventories reached a record high of 1.206 billion barrels in late December and early January.

More than 12 million barrels of stock builds during December occurred in Guangdong province, primarily at state-owned storage facilities linked to Sinopec’s Maoming and PetroChina’s Jieyang refineries, Vortexa said.

Nearly 15 million barrels were accumulated in Shandong. This was in line with record-high sanctioned crude imports into Shandong during November and December, Vortexa added.

“By contrast, imports from Iran fell below 1.3 million bpd in December from more than 1.5 million bpd in November, as increased Russian supplies displaced some Iranian barrels,” said Emma Li, an analyst at Vortexa.

China’s natural gas imports, including pipeline gas and liquefied natural gas (LNG), surged 16.3% from a year earlier in December to 13.45 million tons. For 2025, gas imports totalled 127.87 million tons, down 2.8% from a year earlier, customs data showed.

(1 metric ton of crude oil = 7.3 barrels)

data.title: Key facts: Vedanta Ltd nears demerger approvals; shares hit record high
data.description:
• Vedanta Ltd is nearing final approvals for its demerger into five listed entities, expected to boost shareholder value. Nuvama raised its EBITDA forecasts for 2027 and 2028 by 17% and 8%.1
  • Vedanta Ltd (VEDL) saw a 4.3% increase, achieving a record high, amid a general rise in Indian metal stocks fueled by surging global metal prices.2

data.title: Key facts: SBI Gains Attention for Credit Growth; Macquarie Recommends Shift
data.description:
• State Bank of India (SBI) is a key investment for traders looking to capitalize on expected credit growth ahead of the 2026 Union Budget, especially in financial sectors.1
  • Macquarie suggests a shift to State Bank of India (SBIN) due to its lower valuations and clearer return on equity outlook, amid concerns over deposit growth in other banks.2

data.title: Key facts: Kotak Mahindra Bank shares split; NCD issuance resolution planned
data.description:
• Kotak Mahindra Bank shares began trading on January 14, 2026, after a stock split converting one ₹5 share into five ₹1 shares for eligible shareholders as of January 13.1
  • Kotak Mahindra Bank is set to consider an enabling resolution for the issuance of Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs) through a private placement.2

data.title: Key facts: HCL Technologies reports $3.8B revenue; faces ₹9.6B charge
data.description:
• HCL Technologies reported USD 3.8 billion in revenue for 3QFY26, with a 4.2% quarter-over-quarter growth. The EBIT margin was 18.6%, and new deal TCV reached USD 3.0 billion.123
  • HCL Technologies Ltd (HCLTech) faces a one-time charge of 9.6 billion rupees from new labor codes, impacting EBIT by Rs 956 crore. Hiring plans remain unchanged.45

data.title: Key facts: CLSA targets HDFC Bank at Rs 1,200; solid investment ahead
data.description:
• CLSA targets HDFC Bank at Rs 1,200 per share, highlighting FY27 as a recovery year. The bank trades at a 10-12% price-to-book discount to ICICI Bank, indicating a buying chance.1
  • HDFC Bank is a major player in finance, seen as a solid investment to benefit from credit growth ahead of the Union Budget 2026, despite a limited pre-Budget rally.2

data.title: Key facts: BPCL partners with Indian Oil for exploration; awards Wabag ₹600 crore contract
data.description:
• Bharat Petroleum Corporation Ltd (BPCL) has initiated a joint operation with Indian Oil Corporation to explore the Onshore Block 1 concession, focusing on enhancing exploration activities in the area.1
  • Bharat Petroleum Corp Ltd (BPCL) has awarded VA Tech Wabag a ₹250–₹600 crore contract for advanced water treatment facilities at its Bina Refinery, set for completion in 22 months.2

data.title: South Korea finance minister vows near-term responses to FX volatility
data.description:
South Korea will seek near-term responses to address increasing volatility in the foreign exchange market and find ways to resolve an imbalance in dollar demand and supply, the country's finance minister said on Wednesday.

"Recently, despite record-high current account surpluses, volatility is increasing in the foreign exchange market with one-sided trading," Minister Koo Yun-Cheol said in remarks prepared for a discussion forum on currency market policies.

The won hit on Wednesday its weakest level since December 24 at 1,479.2 per dollar, extending losses for an 11th consecutive session.

Rapidly growing overseas securities investments were the main reason behind the recent imbalance between dollar demand and supply, Koo said.

Koo also said discussions about the currency market impact of the country's pension fund, as well as policy changes to win an upgrade to developed-market status, would be pushed forward in a speedy manner.

data.title: Italy - Factors to watch on January 14
data.description:
The following factors could affect Italian markets on Wednesday.

Reuters has not verified the newspaper reports, and cannot vouch for their accuracy. New items are marked with (*).

For a complete list of diary events in Italy please click on .

POLITICS

Italy, echoing moves by European partners such as Germany, France and Britain, on Tuesday said it had summoned the Iranian ambassador over the violent crackdown of protesters.

ECONOMY

OECD releases December composite leading indicator data (1100 GMT).

COMPANIES

BANCO BPM

The lender is looking to increase seats reserved for minority shareholders as it prepares to appoint a new board, two sources said, after France's Credit Agricole strengthened its ties with the Italian bank.

BANCA IFIS

The lender said on Tuesday it had completed a 400 million euro 10-year Tier 2 subordinated bond issuance with a 4.546% coupon, attracting demand of more than twice the offer.

DIARY

Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni visits Oman.

((Milan newsroom, e-mail: reutersitaly@thomsonreuters.com))

data.title: ZAWYA-PRESSR: National Finance continues advancing Oman’s development agenda through its sustainable finance offering
data.description:
Muscat: Reaffirming its commitment to supporting the nation’s transition toward a low-carbon, future-ready economy, National Finance, the Sultanate of Oman’s leading finance company, continues to enhance its Sustainable Finance offerings as part of its long-standing dedication to responsible and forward-looking financial practices. This focus aligns with the objectives of Oman Vision 2040, particularly in promoting environmentally conscious development across the financial sector. Through these initiatives, National Finance is empowering individuals and businesses to adopt greener technologies, minimize their environmental impact, and contribute to the nation’s long-term sustainability goals.

Speaking on the company’s broader purpose and the role of responsible financing, Mr. Tariq Sulaiman Al Farsi, Chief Executive Officer of National Finance, said: “Sustainability is no longer a peripheral consideration it is central to how nations grow, how industries evolve, and how communities secure long-term wellbeing. At National Finance, we understand that meaningful progress occurs when financial institutions provide practical and accessible pathways that empower individuals and businesses to make forward-looking decisions. Through our Sustainable Finance offerings, we strive to promote positive behavior, accelerate the adoption of greener practices, and enable our customers to actively contribute to the nation’s sustainable future. This reflects both our responsibility and our commitment to driving tangible change.”

National Finance’s Sustainable Finance suite spans its Auto Finance, Consumer Durable Finance, SME, and Corporate segments, providing customers with solutions that deliver tangible value at every stage of their sustainability journey. For individual customers, the offering includes practical financing options that promote the adoption of cleaner technologies and more energy-efficient lifestyles. These solutions encompass electric and hybrid vehicle financing to support the transition to low-emission mobility. Additionally, National Finance offers financing for home solar panel installations, water recycling systems, energy-efficient appliances, and smart-home technologies, making sustainable living increasingly accessible for families across the Sultanate of Oman.

For businesses and SMEs, National Finance provides a comprehensive suite of tailored solutions that enable organizations to integrate sustainability into their operations, investment decisions, and strategic planning. These include financing for renewable energy projects such as solar, wind, and hydro systems, alongside support for eco-friendly real estate developments that adhere to green building standards. The company also offers financing for low-carbon transportation solutions to modernize corporate fleets and promote cleaner mobility, as well as products designed to support sustainable agriculture, waste-reduction initiatives, and recycling innovations. Complementing these offerings, National Finance provides working capital solutions for sustainability-linked enterprises, ensuring that businesses with long-term environmental commitments have the financial flexibility required to achieve their goals. The company also maintains strategic partnerships and collaborations with sustainability-focused companies, including Sheida, the first company to manufacture solar panels, and Muscat Power Projects, a leading provider of solar energy system design and installation services, in addition to several other strategic collaborations.

To further promote the adoption of sustainable solutions, National Finance offers preferential financing rates of up to 1% lower for eligible Sustainable Finance products, along with zero processing fees on approved projects, providing meaningful incentives for individuals and businesses committed to integrating sustainable technologies and practices. Corporate and SME customers also benefit from the guidance of National Finance’s experienced team, who are equipped to help organizations evaluate opportunities, structure sustainability projects, and implement environmentally responsible upgrades with confidence.

National Finance’s Sustainable Finance solutions are fully aligned with modern ESG principles, reinforcing responsible business practices and enabling clients to meet both national priorities and global sustainability expectations. Since embarking on its sustainability journey, the company has consistently integrated Environmental, Social, Operations, and Governance considerations across its operations, establishing a cohesive institutional framework that supports informed decision-making and sustainable value creation. From product design to customer experience and community-focused initiatives, National Finance takes an integrated approach that advances financial progress alongside environmental responsibility.

As interest in responsible finance continues to grow across the Sultanate of Oman, National Finance remains committed to expanding access to sustainable solutions, enhancing customer understanding, and supporting the wider transition toward greener choices. With 24 branches across the nation and an impact-focused advisory approach, the company positions itself as a Partner for Growth, empowering individuals and businesses to make decisions today that contribute to a more sustainable national future.

data.title: Australia's Recce Pharmaceutical hits over 2-yr high on $3.5 million R&D tax rebate
data.description:
** Shares of Recce Pharmaceuticals rise 3% to A$0.69, their highest level since early Sept 2023

** Pharmaceutical co receives cash refund of A$5.3 million ($3.55 million) research and development tax incentive rebate from the Australian Taxation Office

** Stock up 35.3% in 2025

($1 = 1.4932 Australian dollars)

data.title: Australia's Ballymore Resources hits over 4-year high on mineral find in Queensland
data.description:
** Shares of Ballymore Resources advances as much as 19.70% to A$0.395, its highest since early September 2021

** Stock logs biggest intraday pct gain since December 16, 2025

** Co reports strongest intercept yet, flags major find with high-grade Silver, Lead, Zinc and Indium at its Torpy project in Queensland

** Over 601,800 shares change hands, 2.7x the 30-day average volume

** Stock gained 166.7% last year

data.title: Saks Global files for bankruptcy after Neiman Marcus takeover leads to financial collapse
data.description:
High-end department store conglomerate Saks Global filed for bankruptcy protection late on Tuesday in one of the largest retail collapses since the pandemic.

The move cast uncertainty over the future of U.S. luxury fashion barely a year after a takeover that brought Saks Fifth Avenue, Bergdorf Goodman and Neiman Marcus under the same roof.

data.title: ZAWYA-SNG: EIB Global offers $150mln financing for Africa’s largest hybrid solar PV project in Egypt
data.description:
Staff Writer

The European Investment Bank (EIB), through its development arm EIB Global, announced on Tuesday a $150m financing package for Egypt’s Obelisk solar photovoltaic project, coinciding with the inauguration of the first phase of the plant in Qena.

The event was attended by Egyptian Prime Minister Mostafa Madbouly; EIB Global Director General Andrew McDowell; Deputy Prime Minister for Industrial Development and Minister of Transport and Industry Kamel Al-Wazir; Minister of Planning, Economic Development and International Cooperation Rania Al-Mashat; Minister of Electricity and Renewable Energy Mahmoud Esmat; Qena Governor Khaled Abdel Halim; European Union Ambassador to Egypt Angelina Eichhorst; and Scatec CEO Terje Pilskog.

The Obelisk project comprises a 1.1 GWp solar photovoltaic plant combined with a 100 MW/200 MWh battery energy storage system, developed by Scatec, making it the largest hybrid solar PV project in Africa. Electricity generated by the project will be sold to the Egyptian Electricity Transmission Company under a long-term power purchase agreement, supporting a more resilient, diversified and sustainable power system.

The project is being co-financed with the African Development Bank and, under a Team Europe approach, also involves the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD) and British International Investment (BII). Additional support is provided through grants, concessional financing and an EU NDICI guarantee. The coordinated effort underscores Europe’s role as a long-term and reliable partner in Egypt’s green energy transition.

“This landmark project demonstrates how strategic European financing can accelerate Egypt’s clean energy ambitions while strengthening energy security and economic resilience,” said EIB Vice-President Gelsomina Vigliotti. “By supporting the Obelisk solar project, the EIB is investing in large-scale renewable infrastructure that delivers affordable, sustainable power, supports decarbonisation, and reinforces our partnership with Egypt under the European Union’s Global Gateway and the EU-Egypt Strategic Partnership.”

“Scatec is very pleased to welcome the European Investment Bank as a partner in the Obelisk project,” said Terje Pilskog, CEO of Scatec. “Their support is instrumental in driving this landmark initiative forward, and we are proud to be working together to strengthen Egypt’s renewable energy future.”

“The inauguration of Obelisk’s first phase shows what can be achieved when strong national ambition is matched with reliable long-term financing and effective partnerships,” said EIB Global Director General Andrew McDowell. “By supporting Egypt’s first large-scale solar project with battery storage, the EIB is helping to translate climate commitments into concrete infrastructure that delivers clean electricity, builds local capacity and accelerates the energy transition.”

“The European Union stands with Egypt as it accelerates its green transition,” said EU Ambassador to Egypt Angelina Eichhorst. “The Obelisk photovoltaic solar project is a shining example of how our Egyptian partners deliver clean, reliable energy, create skilled jobs and advance climate action, with the EU as a strong partner.”

The Obelisk project directly supports Egypt’s target of sourcing 42% of electricity generation from renewable energy by 2030, while also contributing to EU priorities under REPowerEU and the EU–Egypt Partnership Priorities. It further reflects the EIB’s commitment to advancing climate action, energy security and sustainable development across the Southern Neighbourhood.

data.title: Australia's Advance Metals rises to over 3-month high on gold, silver find at Mexico project
data.description:
** Shares of Advance Metals rise 17.9% to A$0.165, their highest level since October 7

** Stock on track for its strongest trading session since December 29, if gains sustain

** The metal explorer reports silver-gold mineralisation at its Yoquivo project in Mexico

** More than 19.8 million shares change hands, 3.3 times the 30-day average

** Says drilling results show project is a robust, high-grade system with significant room to grow

** Stock rose 341.2% last year
